_meta.purpose: Venue library for Bolo Boys event management. Claude checks this before creating new event entries and updates it when new venues are added.

venues.name: Side Saddle
venues.notes: Regular gig. Monthly showcase. Patio available. Often co-billed with Dirty Shame & Acoustic Station.
venues.summary: A wine saloon along the Atlanta BeltLine in Boulevard Heights — checkered floors, terracotta velvet booths, palm-frond-print chairs, wide-open windows, and a patio that doubles the room when the weather works.
what_we_love: the room's actual personality (checkered floors, terracotta booths)
the funky and sustainable wine list
Thursday-night jazz that sets the tone for the room
venues.live_music_context: Hosts a Thursday-night jazz program. Bolo Boys play a monthly showcase here, often co-billed with Dirty Shame and Acoustic Station.
venues.crowd_notes: Neighborhood wine-bar regulars plus BeltLine walk-ins. Sophisticated menu, unfussy room.
venues.practical_notes: Outdoor patio with significant capacity. BeltLine pedestrian access. Wine-led (orange wines, pet-nats, unusual whites/reds) alongside small bites and a short cocktail list.

venues.name: Cultivation Brewing
venues.notes: Multiple bookings. Annual showcase with Dirty Shame.
venues.summary: Norcross brewery just outside the historic square — indoor and outdoor seating that feels like hanging out in someone's backyard. Cornhole, rotating food trucks, low-key tap list rotation.
what_we_love: the backyard-garden feel of the space
the cornhole game on the patio
the rotating tap list (new beer each week)
the way the indoor/outdoor split actually works
venues.live_music_context: Live music sits in the regular rotation on Friday or Saturday evenings. Bolo Boys have an annual showcase here with Dirty Shame.
venues.crowd_notes: Family-safe brewery crowd, dogs welcome. Mix of Norcross locals and Gwinnett brewery-hoppers.
venues.practical_notes: Dog-friendly. Cornhole and ample outdoor seating. Food trucks typically Wednesday/Thursday evenings and Friday–Sunday. Rotating tap list.

venues.name: Echo Art Gallery
venues.notes: Address/maps link needed — verify before next show listing. Show: 10/18/2025.
venues.summary: Contemporary art gallery housed inside Guardian Studios at Echo Street West — spacious, well-lit rooms with clearly labeled work and comfortable seating, leaning down-to-earth rather than white-cube.
what_we_love: the non-pretentious tone of the space
Echo Street West as a setting for shows
venues.live_music_context: Hosts performances and events alongside its regular art programming.
venues.crowd_notes: Local art-curious crowd, Echo Street West regulars, and small-business supporters. Conversational, not silent-gallery.
venues.practical_notes: Free and open to the public during gallery hours: Wed–Fri 11 AM–5 PM, Sat noon–5 PM. Inside Guardian Studios at Echo Street West.

venues.name: B Complex
venues.notes: Hosted Community Christmas Market. Address/maps link needed.
venues.summary: Cavernous artist-centric space in a repurposed industrial building on Murphy Ave — permanent sculptures, a water feature, and 8,000 square feet of gallery that flexes from exhibition floor to performance venue.
what_we_love: the industrial-cathedral feel of the room
the permanent sculptures and water feature
the artist-cooperative soul of the space
venues.live_music_context: Hosts live performances, art shows, and exhibitions across the year — the room flexes between gallery and performance venue.
venues.crowd_notes: Atlanta arts community — artists, dancers, gallery-goers, the curious. Open to events of many kinds.
venues.practical_notes: 8,000 sq ft exhibition gallery available to rent. Handicap accessible. Street parking near the venue; can fill up during events.

venues.name: Because Coffee
venues.address: 240 Dawson Village Way N #100, Dawsonville, GA 30534
venues.notes: Played 12/13/2025 Christmas show (9 AM–12 PM). Santa in attendance.
venues.summary: Roastery and café in Dawson Village — they roast their beans on-site and the owner-roaster is genuinely fired up about coffee. Indoor and patio seating, public WiFi, a room that flexes from quiet-corner-work to musicians-gathering.
what_we_love: the on-site roast (beans roasted right there)
the seating mix — quiet corners and shareable tables
menu_highlights: seasonal drinks (Luchador, Gimme Samoa, Purple People Eater)
venues.live_music_context: Hosts gatherings of musicians and community events. Bolo Boys played the 2025 Christmas show.
venues.crowd_notes: Dawsonville coffee regulars, work-from-café folks, locals. The owner-roaster is part of the social fabric.
venues.practical_notes: Indoor and outdoor seating. Public WiFi. Hot and cold brew on offer. Family- and meeting-friendly.

venues.name: Wild Heaven Brewing
venues.notes: Ongoing concert series. Typical slots: Fri eve 6:30 PM / Sat afternoon.
venues.summary: Wild Heaven's Avondale Estates taproom and kitchen on Maple Street — indoor seating plus an outdoor section that includes converted-container seating, with a regular live-music schedule across the weekend.
what_we_love: the wide tap list (sours, stouts, IPAs, whatever's pouring)
menu_highlights: kitchen via Al's Pine Street Market
venues.live_music_context: Free live music every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Bolo Boys play here in the ongoing concert series — typical slots Friday evening 6:30 PM or Saturday afternoon.
venues.crowd_notes: Avondale Estates and DeKalb brewery-goers. Multigenerational across the weekend slots; friendly outdoor scene.
venues.practical_notes: Indoor and outdoor seating including converted containers. In-house kitchen (Al's Pine Street Market). Trivia nights and rotating tasting events.

venues.name: Wild Heaven Brewing
venues.address: 2935B N Druid Hills Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30329
venues.notes: Ongoing concert series. Typical slot: Sun afternoon 3–6 PM.
venues.summary: Wild Heaven's Toco Hills taproom and event space, paired with Fox Bros. Bar-B-Q — an onsite brewery, 93-seat taproom, 48-seat event space, indoor TVs, and a courtyard with turf and mature trees built for live music.
what_we_love: the courtyard with mature trees (great for afternoon sets)
the Fox Bros. Bar-B-Q kitchen attached
the patio live-music programming Fri/Sat/Sun (weather permitting)
Tuesday trivia hosted by Dirty South Trivia
venues.live_music_context: Live music on the patio Friday evenings, Saturday afternoons, and Sunday afternoons (weather permitting). Bolo Boys typically play the Sunday afternoon 3–6 PM slot.
venues.crowd_notes: Toco Hills and Druid Hills brewery-goers. Family-friendly courtyard energy, especially in good weather.
venues.practical_notes: 93-seat taproom + 48-seat event space + outdoor courtyard with turf. 3 TVs indoors, brew bar. Weekday specials (½-off cocktails Mondays, ½-off pitchers Wednesdays). Free trivia Tuesdays 7 PM.

venues.name: Gateway Park at Grant Park
venues.notes: Outdoor park. Booked via Grant Park Conservancy. Typical slot: Sun morning 11 AM.
venues.summary: An outdoor stage area inside Atlanta's oldest city park — Boulevard Green sits at a main park entrance, designed for community events like markets, music, kids' activities, and movie nights, with the Milledge Fountain outdoor classroom and theatre nearby.
what_we_love: Atlanta's oldest park as the setting
the Market in the Park crowd
Grant Park Conservancy as a partner
venues.live_music_context: Hosts the Market in the Park series and other Grant Park Conservancy community events; live music is part of the regular Sunday rhythm at Boulevard Green.
venues.crowd_notes: Grant Park families, BeltLine walk-ups, market-goers, and the neighborhood's Sunday-morning regulars. All ages.
venues.practical_notes: Outdoor / weather-dependent. Free and open to all ages. Booked via Grant Park Conservancy. Typical slot Sunday morning 11 AM.

venues.name: Grant Central Pizza East
venues.notes: Pizza restaurant in East Atlanta Village. First show 5/25/2026.
venues.summary: East Atlanta Village pizza spot at the Glenwood corner — hand-tossed NY-style crust, homemade sauce, build-your-own pies, and a casual room that works for groups and families.
venues.live_music_context: Bolo Boys' first booking here was 5/25/2026. Venue hosts occasional live music alongside its standing pizza-shop programming.
venues.crowd_notes: East Atlanta Village locals, families with kids, and groups. Casual, moderately loud, easy to hang.
venues.practical_notes: Limited parking behind the venue. Dine-in casual. Family- and group-friendly.

venues.name: Ideal Sports Bar
venues.notes: New venue as of 2026-05. First booking: June 26 + July 11, 2026. Official brand is 'Ideal Sportsbar' (one word); we normalize to 'Ideal Sports Bar' across the site.
venues.summary: A Lakewood Heights neighborhood bar with dive-bar bones and a family-friendly room — two pool tables inside, a rustic beer garden out back, and bartenders who actually know who's at the bar.
venues.live_music_context: Live bands sit in the regular rotation alongside bingo, karaoke, and holiday nights.
venues.crowd_notes: Multigenerational regulars. The kind of room where the bartender's part of the conversation, not just the order.
venues.practical_notes: Two pool tables inside, beer garden in back. Everyday food and drink prices run comfortable. Family-friendly.
